entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear Mr.David,

Thank you for your program and for the opportunity. Its great initiative by SAP, i would really appreciate if you can recognise me with my work and eagerness to learn new things. 

I identified as a person for this certification because I am a woman, first generation college graduate in my family, People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts. 

I havebeen  learning alot from SAP learning portal.  Have some badges for course completion (TS410).  This opportunity will definitely aid me in up skilling myself and make me very much productive for the company. I am learning about SAP FICO.

Overall, I'm eager to continue developing my skills, and I think this initiative is the perfect way to do so.

Thanks & Regards,
Hana

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone (SAP BTP, SAP Build Work Zone sta...

entry.title: Re: Focused Build- Checklist Functionality Improvements and Decision Based Checklist
entry.content:
Hello Vasundhra, 

Thanks for this blog.

We understand that we can cancel /Reject one of the checklist steps in ST-OST SP Level 14 which is very useful feature. 

Although one question, can we reuse the "Rejected" step to open again and complete it respectively?

Also, we are on SP13, so do we need to wait for this change till further upgrade or it can be done via single note implementation, if yes, what is respective note number.

Regards,

Shivani.

entry.title: Re: New version of RAP Generator available
entry.content:
Hi Andre, very interesting project

I want to ask, if I want to create a program to use the Purchase Requisition API (Example), is there any best suggestion? I assume the user can create a purchase requisition header and item with the condition that the item will be saved as a draft first, several times I tried it still failed, and I haven't found a solution regarding this

entry.title: Re: Delegated User Administration Using Attribute-Based Access Control in SAP Cloud Identity Service
entry.content:
hi @ValAtanassov ,

based on https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/manage-users-rights, supported values for "user.attributes" includes "loginName", "firstName", "lastName" etc. But in this blog "userName" is used instead of "loginName". 

So could you please check whether the attribute names provided in the SAP help link is correct? I believe correct attribute values are userName, name.givenName, name.familyName etc. 

Regards,

Binson

entry.title: Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content:
That's for the Report Header, what happens if you set it on the report Footer only?

What SP are you using? You can find the Version in Programs and Features.

entry.title: Missing User and Authorization Details in SAP SOS Analysis
entry.content:
When I ran a Security Optimization Service (SOS) analysis in SAP Solution Manager, I noticed that important details about users and their authorizations were missing from the report. This makes it difficult for me to fully understand the security of the system.

Main Problems I Encountered:

  1. Missing Named User List: The report didn’t show all the users with critical permissions, like SAP_ALL, which are essential to monitor.
  2. Incomplete Authorization Details: Some important permissions, especially for sensitive functions, weren’t fully captured in the report.
  3. Unclear Configuration: I wasn’t sure how to configure the analysis to properly track all relevant users and their permissions.

This incomplete report is a potential security risk because it prevents me from effectively monitoring users with critical access and ensuring the system is secure.

entry.title: Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content:
Hi Don,

I have report footer a and report footer b.  The report footer A prints along with page header and details as page 1 and report footer B is where I added the terms.  If I supress report footer b the first page print without any 2nd blank page but when I dont supress Report Footer b where I have terms this 3rd page starts printing.

entry.title: Default Attachment Document Type - Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
We would like to set the default Attachment Document Type for Purchase Requisitions to Internal Use (SL9). 

We have not found any Configuration options so for in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Has anyone been able to set this?

entry.title: Re: Stop Production Order Release if PRT Missing
entry.content:
Hello, 

Have you tried OPJK

There is a No Check checkbox, and also for collective conversion it says No Creation if PRT missing. I dont have an example ready with PRT so could test, maybe you can check this.

entry.title: Re: Negative sign in front for Numeric Data Type
entry.content:
Don't use obscure and unsupported function modules (e.g. don't use CLOI_PUT_SIGN_IN_FRONT).

The solution is pure ABAP language since ABAP 7.02, twenty years ago (see the ABAP documentation of string templates - formatting of numbers😞

 

DATA(string) = |{ number }|.

entry.title: Re: MTO Requirements
entry.content: Hi Kaushik, were you able to find a solution ?

entry.title: Same product with mixed batch to be stored in Addition to Existing stock/Next Empty Bin
entry.content:
While Doing the Configuration for Putaway rule "2" with Mixed storage on product Level is not Working .

Where on the Basis of Capacity check the Stock first should be Placed in the bin where Storage is available as per capacity else will be stored in Next empty Bin.
While Following the Configuration suggested in the SAP Note 2409948 Mixed storage of different batches in same bin not working, Getting the Error Mixed storage in HU only with mixed storage in bin (Message No. /SCWM/LT275).

entry.title: Re: BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE is working in SE37 but is only partially working through program
entry.content: Following Raymond explanations, when running the test via SE37, see the entered values by debug at the beginning of the function module, you will see how they have been converted by the conversion exits.

entry.title: Re: How to find the delivery with billing document reversed
entry.content:
Hi,

In your case you try with below two options

1) Fetch record from VBFA and input original invoice number in VBELV -Preceding sales and distribution document and
find cancel document number from VBELN - Subsequent sales and distribution document field.

2) Take a help of ABAPER and create new report with using VBFA.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: DRC - no data selected by "Switzerland VAT return (CH...
entry.content: Hi Umasaral,

entry.title: can we have trail version to connect to cloud connector for on-premise system
entry.content: we have trail version to connect to cloud connector for on-premise system

entry.title: Re: Fetching wrong exchange rate from tcurr in fm CONVERT...
entry.content:
Hi, I am posting the solution incase anyone facing same issue.

So .877 is a indirect exchange rate. Go to se16n and check data, you will see '/ ' before indirect exchange rate value. 

Incase of indirect exchange rate you read it from right to left. So 1EUR = 0.877GBP. In se11 contents this is not clear.

entry.title: Re: DRC - no data selected by "Switzerland VAT return (CH...
entry.content: 1. Verify Configuration: Check the DRC customization settings, especially for tax codes and reporting entity assignments. Done already before - mentioned above 2. Check Tax Code Mapping: Ensure all tax codes are correctly mapped in your settings. Done already before - mentioned above - but does it not throw errors when a mapping is missing? 3. Access CDS Views: Use transaction `SE11` to review the `I_STRPTAXITEM` CDS view and confirm the expected data is present. Done already before - mentioned above 4. Review DRC Logs: Check transaction `SLG1` for application logs related to DRC. Look for any warnings or errors. I will have a look, but I expect to see errors in the dashboard. It would be strange if SAP would hide them elsewhere 5. Inspect DRC Dashboard: Ensure you are selecting the correct reporting period and entity when generating the VAT return. Done already before - mentioned above 6. Debugging Tools: Use transaction `SE80` to navigate the DRCrelated programs. Set breakpoints to analyze data selection processes. I don't know the programs.... if you can tell me the right ones, I will debug them. 7. Compare with Standard Report: Run the standard VAT report (RFUMSV00) and compare the output with what is expected in DRC. Done already before - mentioned above 8. Data Validation: Create a custom report or use queries to crosscheck accounting documents from tables like BKPF and BSEG against DRC criteria. Does not make sense, for this we can use RFUMSV00 9. Check for SAP Notes: Search the SAP Support Launchpad for notes related to DRC issues for known fixes or patches. Done already 10. Consult SAP Support: If issues persist, reach out to SAP Support for further assistance.

entry.title: Re: Professional Services in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release
entry.content: Hello @KatharinaStopf, what process or action must be carried out to say that the product that I have chosen in the work package based on its use begins to be taken as consumed?

entry.title: New Election SAP Academic Board DACH 2024-2026
entry.content:
Following the Academic Community Conference DACH 2024 in Vienna we are exited to introduce the newly elected Academic Board of committed faculty. In our role we are committing on enforcing the collaboration inside of the Academic Community and between industry and academia to ensure for the next generation to be equipped with the knowledge and skills that are required to meet today’s requirements and tackle future challenges.

 

For the upcoming term we want to focus on Sustainability and Artificial Intelligence as significant pillars across the domains of process, technology, digital transformation and management, teaching methods as well as international collaboration.

While we redefined our focus, our mission remains the same:

  • We promote networking and the exchange of experiences between learners, teachers and researchers in the context of business, processes and IT. The exchange is promoted by organizing and implementing joint events.
  • We bring together interested and motivated experts from different subject areas and specialist areas.
  • We provide the framework for determining the needs of teachers/learners and companies.
  • We work together with SAP and the Competence Centers on a financeable use of the offers.
  • We promote further training and networking between teachers and young talents in cooperation with the SAP community.

Here you can find these years defined workgroups and the responsible lead:

Sustainability (cross-sectional, all)

Artificial Intelligence (cross-sectional, all)

Business Process Management ( @hscheruhn  Hans Scheruhn; @ Jan Mendling ; @ Martin Adam)

Technology (@ Gard Sébastien; @ Sebastian Baltes; @stefan_stoeckler  Stefan Stöckler)

Digital Transformation and Management (@ Peter Langkafel; @umathis Uta Mathis; @ Stefan Renggli)

Teaching Methods ( @birgit_lankes    Birkgit Lankes; @cordula_boden Cordula Boden, @ Tim Neumann)

International Collaboration (@ Jorge Marx-Gomez; @umathis  Uta Mathis; @hscheruhn Hans Scheruhn)

entry.title: Re: Due date
entry.content: Did you check if users do change in the document manually, also did you already look for customer exits, BAdI, BTE of Substituion rules in your system, look also for recent import of some Customizing Tables.

entry.title: Re: Delivery priority in SAP TM
entry.content:
Hi Tural,
you can use the costs for non-delivery as priority. Orders with high priority should have high costs. A BADI is not needed, you can set it on the UI via conditions.
In addition you have to set the following blackboard param in rcc_param:
Name: OrderNonDeliveryCostSorting
Section: VSR_ELS
Switch: Integer
Integer: 2
With this param you can influence the sorting in which the optimizer plans the orders. But be aware that there is no guarantee that an order with higher priority will always be planned instead of one with lower priority. The goal of VSR is to reduce the overall costs.

Best regards,
Kathrin

entry.title: Re: LOCATE Original User - Table BKPF: USNAM = SAP_WFRT a...

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Adding Background Image to All Tiles in Workzone Advance Page
entry.content:
I have a requirement to add a background image to all tiles on the Workzone Advance page using Theming.

I attempted to add the image through **/comsapuitheming.themedesigner/**, and while it works, I encountered an issue. The ID for the tile is not fixed. If my application has two pages, the background image is applied to the first tile of whichever page loads first.

Additionally, all static tiles created in Workzone do not have specific IDs associated with the names given to them, which complicates applying the image to individual tiles using css.

Could you please assist with this issue?

entry.title: Re: Role design strategy for SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Very nice guide to understand a little better how Role Designing works in SAP Public Cloud.

In your Limitation section you mention the "Inability to separate authorizations from the role menu to create task or enabler roles". Does that mean that the values Write, Read, Value Help will apply to all the applications included in the same catalog ?
Let me explain myself with an example:

The catalog "SAP_MM_BC_PC_PROCESS_MC" include many applications. Two of those are "Change Purchase Contract" and "Create Purchase Contract". Is there a way to limit the use of the app "create purchase Contract" to only display information (without modification) and at the same time allow modification in the "Change Purchase COntract" application ? 
I understand that this is not possible and that the only way to limite the usage of an app within a catalog would be setting up some specific restrictions (by company or purchasing groupe for instance) to allow the user to only Create some type of contracts. Is that correct ?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Retry method when encountering "connection reset by peer" for HTTP receiver channel
entry.content: In a sync process that fails the client should be the one to initiate a retry then.

entry.title: Re: Standard API to fetch Shipping Point Address details of Outbound Delivery
entry.content: Any Updates?

entry.title: Consuming Data from Datasphere to Azure Data Factory via ODBC
entry.content:
Prerequisites:

  • Access: Access to ADF and Datasphere.
  • Credentials: Datasphere and ADF credential details.

Connect Datasphere to Azure Data Factory

DATASPHERE PART:

  • Log in to Datasphere -> Space Management -> Choose the space and select Edit

     

  • Click Create and Make sure that you have enabled Expose for consumption by default

  • Copy the Database Username, Hostname, Port, Password

     

  • Go to System-> Configuration-> IP Allowlist-> Trusted Ips
    EXTERNAL IPV4 ADDRESS should be added here, not Internal IPV4

     

  • To get an External IPV4 Address, use this URL:  What Is My IP Address - See Your Public Address - IPv4 & IPv6
  • Add and Save the External ipv4 address in the Datasphere’s IP Allowlist.

ODBC PART:

  • Need to install SAP HDODBC driver SAP Development Tools (ondemand.com) in the system.
  • Open ODBC in the system
  • Click Add
  • Select HDODBC

     

     

    • Give any meaningful name to Data source name, description.
    • Database type: SAP HANA Cloud or SAP HANA Single tenant (both will work fine).
    • Already copied Host URL in datasphere space, Paste the copied Host URL.
    • Click Test connection
    • Paste the Database username in the Username and Password

       

       

       

AZURE DATA FACTORY PART:

Open Azure Data Factory:

  • Go to your Azure Data Factory instance via the Azure Portal.

Create a Linked Service:

  • On the left pane, go to Manage > Linked services.
  • Click New to create a new Linked Service.
  • In the search box, search for ODBC or SAP HANA.
  • Select ODBC

 

Enter Connection Information:

  • In the Connection String field, enter the connection string:

Driver={HDBODBC};ServerNODE=XXXXXXXXXX:443;UID=SAP_CONTENT#XXXX;PWD=XXXXXXXXXXXX;SCHEMA=SAP_CONTENT;

Breakdown of Components:

  • Driver: Specifies the SAP HANA ODBC driver needed to connect (e.g., {HDBODBC}).
  • ServerNODE: Indicates the SAP HANA server address and port to connect to (e.g., rw2922...443).
  • UID: The username used to authenticate to SAP HANA (e.g., SAP_CONTENT#XXXX).
  • PWD: The password associated with the provided username for authentication.
  • SCHEMA: The specific database schema where the data is located (e.g., SAP_CONTENT).
  • Encrypt: Ensures the connection is encrypted for secure communication (e.g., Encrypt=1).

Authentication Type:

  • Choose Basic Authentication since you’re using a username and password.
  • Enter the copied Datasphere’s Space username and password.

Test the Connection:

  • Click on the Test Connection button to make sure the connection is successful.

 

  • To check the connection, create a copy pipeline and choose the ODBC as the connector which we used to get data from Datasphere and corresponding Destination environment.

 

  • In this case, I have chosen the Azure Data Lake Storage Gen 2 as the Destination environment.

  • Login to Azure Data Lake Storage via Azure platform and check that the data copied

 

  • Hence the connection is established successfully from Datasphere to Azure Data Factory.

HINTS: 😀🤫

If your connection failed, then you have to check these two things,

  1. Make sure your current IPV4 is added to Datasphere's IP Allowlist.
  2. Ensure you have entered the correct Datasphere's Space credentials in your systems ODBC and test connection.

Thank you!

entry.title: How to Trigger a PPF action to Print Handling unit label from fiori/API
entry.content:
Hi members,

My requirement is a web API where we need to trigger a PPF to Print Handling Unit Label and to create a spool for each request that is processed and also, we need to have the reprint option also.

Please let me know how I can achieve it and if you can share anything related references.

Thanks,
Bharath

entry.title: Re: Changing Background colour and width/height of book p...
entry.content:
(1) Assuming you mean the background color of the page itself (and not the gray area behind it) - you need to activate the Image property (near the top of the property sheet) - don't specify an image, but it does need to be activated for the background color to show. (Yes, it's probably a bug, but it's been like that since forever.)

(2) I'd guess that the Book Page currently has an object that is full-width and/or full-height (or multiple objects with a combined width/height...), and SAP Enable Now won't let you make the page too small to accommodate the content on it.

entry.title: Re: Role design strategy for SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Simon,

You are correct. In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, we can restrict access based on organizational values, such as Company Code. However, it’s important to note that the system does not support granular restrictions for specific actions like create, change, update, download, upload, or lock. When you assign write access, it automatically enables all actions related to write permissions.

You can, however, create app variants and publish them in separate catalogs with similar restriction types. Regarding the Limitation section, "Inability to separate authorizations from the role menu to create task or enabler roles," I meant that in On-premise systems, you can manually add authorization objects to a role without needing to add a catalog. This feature, which allows you to manually add restriction types, is unavailable in the Public Cloud.

I hope this clarifies things. Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards,
Dhanshree.

entry.title: C4C Create Employees and Business Users using Odata
entry.content:
Hi,

In C4C is it possible to create employees and business users in C4C via odata?  If so, which services can be used for this and how would the passwords be set.

(Our use case is to be able to create users automatically as a result of a user request form being filled in - the application for the form would be done by if there are odata services that can support this).  Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Stop create certificate in QC51 before Inspection lot...
entry.content:
Hi Soumy,

 

The inbound certificate receipt functionality is enabled in the material master QM view by the settings of 'QM in procurement' and the 'QM control key'.

So please uncheck the 'QM in procurement'  checkbox in the material master to de-activate the certificate requirement for a material.

entry.title: Who owns Public Cloud Custom Developments?
entry.content:
Hi,

Example: Customer is using a public cloud solution such as Ariba or SFSF. They do some customization. All what they do sits in their respective public tenant, which is exclusive to customer.

From a legal poiunt of view, who owns these customizations done in a public cloud tenant? These are developments done with the customer effort and resources. Meanwhile contract is valid, is there a General Terms and Conditions clause or any public terms and conditions documents that addresses whose intellectual property are the custom developments in public cloud?

We need to clarify this for an auditory process one of the customers is going through. 

Appreciate some help,

Thanks

There is also a nice blog to analytic model and variables: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-datasphere-analytic-model-series-using-variables-in-analytic-model/ba-p/13552802

entry.title: Why does Mig Object classification - Material (MARA) fail with "Assignment exists and is valid" ?
entry.content:
In migration object “Object classification - Material (MARA)”, I completed upload file, prepare and mapping tasks.

However, in simulation, the error message  "Assignment exists and is valid" appears , why ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Why does Mig Object classification - Material (MARA)...
entry.content:
Please refer to the KBA  Error ''Assignment exists and is valid' when using the Migration Object 'Object Classification 'to explain

The Migration Tool failed because there is already Classification for the object ( for example material  ) and class is already assigned.

This tool can work only on Objects without any Classification, by design.

 

If updating Material Master Classification , please check also the Blog How to mass update Material Classification in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Unable to perform POST from SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing error while performing  a POST request form SAP build apps to SAP Hana DB. The service was created using a CAPM project in SAP BTP.

Service works correctly using a postman. GET, POST, PATCH is working.

 

Below is  error "msg":"400 > /n code: '400",In message: 'Property I"@odata.type\" does not exist in Asp•••••••••••••.Asp●●●●●●●●"In)","type":"log"]

 

However, not from build apps.

entry.title: Re: Synchronizing Attributes from Azure to IAS
entry.content:
Hi @gregorw 

provisioning the manager ID is now working.

One more question. 

I have set up the provisioning of users from Entra ID to IAS according to this guide https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/microsoft-entra-id?locale=en-US

I was now able to transfer some user information to IAS, such as User ID, Global User ID, SCIM ID, Email, Name, and Manager.

However, I am unable to provision attributes like Cost Center, Company State/Province, Company Street Address, Company ZIP/Postal Code, ZIP/Postal Code, City, etc.

How can I provision these other attributes?

How can I restrict fields with dropdown menu, such as 'Country', to only one country?"

 

Where can I find information on this?

Thank you very much for any tips

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Default Attachment Document Type - Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
HI @dlindsey 

 

In S/4HANA CLoud there is Harmonized Document Management (HDM)

When it is enabled for an object ( like Purchase Requisitions ) it is possible to set a default document type

using configuration activity Maintain Default and Restricted Document Types

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: UDF and FMS not working
entry.content:
Hi,

Try this

select T0.U_DEFECT, T0.DistNumber,T0.ItemCode, T1.Quantity 
from OBTN T0 inner join OBTQ T1 on T0.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode and T0.SysNumber=T1.SysNumber 
where T1.Quantity>0 and T0.ItemCode=$[IGE1.ItemCode]

Hope this helps,

SonTran

Thanks in advance for your feedback!

entry.title: Re: IW38 ,Display number of entries (Counter) in PM list...

Hi ,

 

Please find attached the layout from IW38 t code.

entry.title: Re: 405 Method Not Allowed when trying HTTP method POST in SAP Gateway Client
entry.content: show us your post call payload

entry.title: Re: 405 Method Not Allowed when trying HTTP method POST in SAP Gateway Client
entry.content: and url for the post call

entry.title: Re: Story reports showing time values one hour prior to actual time values
entry.content:
Hi,
I am experiencing the same issue with the only difference, that I have a difference of 2 hours. I suspect that it has to do with the time zone, as the times have been entered for GMT +2 but the SAP SuccessFactors platform works with the UTC time zone.
@DineshDeogharehave you found a solution in the meantime?

Thanks and kind regards,

Philippe

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content: Hi @raymond_giuseppi, I am checking your mentioned points and get back with the answers.

entry.title: Prevent Religion Key on Holiday Calendar from not generating the holiday on personal calenar
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

When we're maintaining the holiday calendar we can select the religion denomation for each holiday. The standard that i have tested work like this : when the personal calendar is generated, the holiday will only apply to employees that have matching religion key on IT0002 as that holiday, while it won't appear for other employees that have different religious denomination key.

How do i prevent this? i want holiday with religion key A to appear for all employee regardless of their religion.

We're planning to use the religious denomination key for different rate of overtime based on their religion. And the holiday class for automatic generation of absences on time evaluation.

Thanks and regards,

Kelvin

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello David,

It's very happy to receive your information. I am an U40 woman who have decided to have career change and found that SAP consultant field is fit to my desire. I hope to receive this support on my journey with SAP.

Thank you very much 😊

entry.title: Stash-based Lazyloading using ObjectPageLazyLoader
entry.content:
I've implemented stash-based lazyloading of my different tabs using ObjectPageLazyLoader,

and it's working fine the first time the user enters the Object page of my List Report app. However, if the user navigates back to the list page and picks another item to go to the Object page, all the tabs that were previously navigated to (and loaded lazily) now get loaded all together right at the start.

How do I make the lazy-loading reinitiate when the Object page is hit again with a different item?

Thanks

<uxap:ObjectPageSection "idItinExpSec"... >
   <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection id="idItinExpSubsec" ... >
	<uxap:ObjectPageLazyLoader id="idItinExpLazy" stashed="true">
.
.
.

entry.title: Re: Integration SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition - Outbound Delivery is not reflected in SAP CI
entry.content: Hello, I would want to know if there is a solution found for this problem Juan Jara

entry.title: Re: How can I add more Collaboration Links in DMC and Col...
entry.content: Hi Mike, thanks for your answer.

entry.title: Reverse engineer from XSD file, and generating XSM model, how to populate Attribute comment in PDM?
entry.content:
Hi, I have used the "Reverse engineer->XML Definition" and this way imported XSD files (defining ISO messages) into PowerDesigner 16.5 (DataArchitect license).

Then I used "Tools->Generate Physical Data Model".

The XSD Complex Type contains the attribute comments as below on Documentation:

<xs:element name="_baseType" minOccurs="0" type="xs:string">
   <xs:annotation>
      <xs:appinfo>Schema for processing submitted applications</xs:appinfo>
      <xs:documentation>When sub-classing, this defines the super-class</xs:documentation>
   </xs:annotation>
</xs:element>

MY issue is, while generating Physical Data Model, my expectation is that this Documentation info should be available on attribute comment in PDM.

Any hint on this on how to achieve it?

entry.title: Create Multiple HU for 1 Qty with UOM SET/EA in Inbound delivery
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a business requirement as explained below:

Our customer wants to receive the Material with UOM 'SET' of Qty 1 packed in the 5 carton and stored in the 5 different HU's. And also while goods issue to the sales order 5HU needs to be picked for the delivery.

Example: Sofa Set with packed into the 5 different packing.

In the SAP, Inbound delivery process system allow us to create 1 HU for 1 Set. 

It would be great if someone has any idea about this issue or whether this is feasible in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How can I add more Collaboration Links in DMC and Col...
entry.content: Hi Mike, thanks for your answer. We added all Communication Arrangements, but only see 4 Collaboration Links and 2 Directives. We are missing for example: COLLABORATION_ACTIVITY_CONFIRMATION_DI or COLLABORATION_GOODS_RECEIPT_CONF_DI

entry.title: Re: Position of Block
entry.content:
for question 1:

- not aware of options to move blocks / areas in selection screen definition except you build additional a manually designed screen using screen painter.

- but what can be done is to put blocks into blocks, that moves the inner level block slightly to the right.

entry.title: Include Workflow tasks in authorization role on productive system
entry.content:
Hello,

we built a new authorization role on the S/4 development system including several workflow tasks.
After transport of the role into our test system, the workflow components were not included in the role anymore and had to be added manually in PFCG.

This is okay for the test system. But our authorizations are strictly limited on the productive system and no one is allowed to change roles on the productive system directly.

How can we achive a transport of the role including the workflow tasks, or is there any other solution to add these to the role?

Thanks,

Julia

entry.title: Re: anybody tried the learning hub IDES yet?
entry.content: I haven't even noticed it's there! sounds like a good option for public cloud. It gives you access for a limited time though

entry.title: Re: Religion Validation in Time Schema
entry.content:
Found a workaround for this, we used the holiday class as the religion flag

4 - Islam

5 - Christian

6 - Buddhist

7 - Hinduism

Then wrote a PCR that would compare the holiday class with the religion field on IT0002, you can call the table using the operation TABLEP0002 and the field name using OUTVX***** with KONFE being the field name for religion key

 

Hope this help anyone facing the same requirement

entry.title: SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 integration Adobe Sign: roadmap
entry.content:
Hi,

on the SAP CPQ Quote 1.0 vs 2.0 feature differences help page (Quote 1.0 vs Quote 2.0 - Feature Differences | SAP Help Portal ), the integration with AdobeSign is listed as "To do". Is there any idea when this will be on the roadmap/become available?

Best,

Vincent

entry.title: Documentation on strategyForVisibilityChange and lazyLoading
entry.content:
Can someone please provide some information on the Strategy For Visibility? I've seen the following code snippet in some UI5 apps but I can't find any documentation on what it does and how it works:

<uxap:customData>
   <core:CustomData key="strategyForVisibilityChange" value="lazyLoading"/>
</uxap:customData>

Would appreciate any help.

entry.title: Re: Release Notes for SQL Anywhere 17.0 SP1 PL17 Build 78...
entry.content: The release notes should not be accessible externally. If you have any issues, please post here.

entry.title: Re: How can I see a repot that shows depreciations to be...
entry.content:
This information should already be available (since 2020) read:

  • Customer influence : Future Planned Depreciation reporting - online help 
  • Planning Horizon : Evaluation of Asset Values from Future Fiscal Years (Planning Horizon)

entry.title: Billbacks Handling via Settlement Management
entry.content:
Hi - How to handle bill backs in S4HANA Settlement Management? Do we create a Purchase Rebates to handle this? How to handle the incoming claims?

Reg

Siva

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 SQL Dashboard User name and password not working
entry.content: Hi @nidhisingh14 , Issue is different. It is not relevant with SAP Note 3471145

entry.title: Scheduling in SAP PP - Planned order vs Production order - Lead time vs Base date
entry.content:
Intro:

Lets see the scheduling in SAP PP. This may be useful for someone. We should not get confused between planned order scheduling and production order scheduling.

The scheduling happening in MRP run are relevant only to Planned orders. This is planned order scheduling.

When we convert the planned order to production order an another set of scheduling will happen. This is production order scheduling.

Overview: 

I) Scheduling types for planned orders:

I.1. Basic date scheduling

I.1.a) Lot independent basic date scheduling

I.1.b) Lot dependent Basic date scheduling

I.2. Lead time scheduling

II) Scheduling types for production orders:

II.1. Detailed Lead time scheduling

Terms you need to be aware.

Opening date - This will be available only in planned order. Also its just reference field and will not affect scheduling and dependent requirements of planned orders at all. Its just a time frame given to planner to convert the planned order to production order. Usually planner will open CO41 / COHV report based on opening date to covert the planned orders.

Opening date = Planned order start date - Opening period.

Release date - This will be available only in the production order. Also its just reference field and will not affect scheduling at all. Its just a time frame which is used by planner to filter the production orders in COHV report to filter and release the production orders.

Release date = Production order scheduled start date - Release period

Float before and float after production will actually affect the scheduling in planned orders (Only lead time scheduling) and production orders.

Order start date = start date in planned order or basic start date in production order.

Order end date = end date in planned order or basic end date in production order. 

Production start date in planned order = scheduled start date in production order

Production end date in planned order = Scheduled end date in Production order

Note: Get familiarize with the dates by seeing the dates in MD13 and CO03 tcodes.

Prerequisites:

I have created a flow of creating master data, PIR, MRP run, and conversion of planned order to production order. Will explain the types of scheduling (both planned and production orders) on the flow.

OPU5 - Planned order configs

Here the adjust dates field to be mentioned as above. So the system will dynamically change the planned order start and end date according to the situation.

OPU3 - Production order configs

Here the scheduling check box is always checked. Production orders and process orders are scheduled at a detailed planning level using the routing or master recipe. The adjust dates to be set as above. So the dates in operation can be dynamically change according to the capacity plan and schedules.

Master data - MRP1 view:

Here in schedule margin key Opening period = 10 days, Float before production = 1 day, Float after production = 2 day, Release period = 5 days.

Master data - Routing: 3 operations with 8 hrs. Total 24 hrs in same work center.

Master data - Work center: 8hr working per day.

So the WC has to work 24 hr to produce 1 pc and it will take 3 day to complete it as per above routing.

Master data - BOM:

Main part: Backward scheduling only discussed

I.1.a) Lot independent basic date scheduling: Planned orders

This scheduling mode uses only uses the data mentioned in material master and will not consider the routing data. So this mode will not consider the order quantity. Also this will affect the Inhouse and external product differently.

In MD01 itself its mentioned that its for planned orders.

Material master fields considered for scheduling are In house production time & GR processing time in MRP 2 view

Scenario : After MRP run scheduling mode 1 - basic date scheduling - lot independent

I.1.a.a) Inhouse production material - MD04 screen for header material

Note: In calendar you can see the italic days and red highlighted days are holidays.

 Note: Use this pic to go from back date to front date

Requirement date = 04.10.2024 As per configuration the scheduling will be takes place backwards

Planned order end date = Requirement date - GR processing time (working days) = 04.10.24 - 1 = 02.10.24

Planned order start date = Planned order end date - In-house production time (working days) = 02.10.24 - 1 (03.10.24 Holiday) = 01.10.24

Opening date = Plnd Order start date - Opening period (Working days) = 01.10.24 - 10 = 17.09.24 (dummy date no affect on scheduling)

Available for MRP = Planned order end date + GR processing time = 02.10.24 + 1 day = 04.10.2024.

Here the scheduling margin key have no effect on lot independent scheduling. Now lets convert the planned order to production order. Here comes another topic.

II.1) Production order Scheduling - Lead time scheduling.

Even though we done basic date scheduling for planned orders when we convert planned order to production order the system will do lead time production scheduling by considering the routing data.

Here a question may rise - Then why basic date scheduling in planned order? Please comment your opinion.

Note: Here you have to consider the start time and end time of work center to calculate the dates

Now system determines the start date of production order as its backward scheduling.

Note: In the pic start from basic end date and go till release date to understand the calculation

Basic end date = picked from end date of planned order = 02.10.2024

Scheduled end date = Basic end date - float after production = 02.10.2024 - 1 = 30.09.23

Scheduled start date = Scheduled end date - Routing data = 30.10.24 - 6 (2pc*3days) = 23.09.24 

Basic start date = Scheduled start date - Float before production = 23.09.24 - 2 = 19.09.24

Release date = Scheduled start date - release period = 23.09.24 - 5 = 16.09.24 (just time No affect on scheduling)

Note: The basic date in production order is getting updated as per new scheduling. The work center start and end time to be considered during calculation.

I.1.a.b) External procurement material - Lot independent Scheduling

Here the component is external material. So the material master date considered for scheduling are Planned deliver time, GR processing time in MRP2 view and Purchase processing time from configs (OPPQ).

Here you can see the planned order start date of header material (01.10.24) is moved as dependent requirement date to the component and not the opening period (17.09.24).

Planned dates = Requirement date = 01.10.24

Delivery date (end) = Planned date - GR processing date (working day) = 01.10.24 - 1 = 30.09.24

Release date (Start) = Delivery - Purchasing processing time (OPPQ) (Working days) - Planned delivery time (Calendar days) = 01.10.24 - 1 - 5 (Incl holidays) = 24.09.24

I.1.b) Lot dependent Basic date scheduling

Here the System will consider the order quantity to do scheduling.

You can update these values by running the report CA97N. Based on routing values the report will update the fields accordingly.

Scenario : After MRP run scheduling mode 1 - basic date scheduling - lot dependent

I.1.b.a) Inhouse production material - MD04 screen for header material

Material master data considered for calculating the start and end date of planned order are Setup time, Processing time, Interoperation time, Base quantity in the Work scheduling view and GR processing time.

Note: Here the processing time is only let dependent. Interoperation and processing time are default values.

Requirement date = 04.10.2024. As per configuration the scheduling will be takes place backwards

Planned order end date = Requirement date -  GR processing time (wrk day) = 04.10.24 - 1 = 02.10.24

Planned order start date = Plan order end date - (Processing time * order quantity) (wrk day) + interoperation time (wrk day) + setup time (wrk day)  = 02.10.24 - (1*2) + 1 + 2 = 04.10.24 - 5 = 25.09.24

Opening date = Plnd Order start date - Opening period (Working days) = 25.09.24 - 10 = 11.09.24 (dummy date no affect on scheduling)

I.1.b.b) External procurement - there are no changes as there are no work scheduling view in raw materials

Planned dates = Requirement date = 25.09.24

Delivery date (end) = Planned date - GR processing date (working day) = 25.09.24 - 1 = 24.09.24

Release date (Start) = Delivery date - Purchasing processing time (OPPQ) (Working days) - Planned delivery time (Calendar days) = 24.09.24 - 1 - 5 (Incl holidays) = 18.09.24

I.2) Lead time scheduling: Planned order

Here the system will consider the routing data to determine the planned start and end date of planned order. But even though it will not do capacity planning see . This method is useful if you use capacity leveling at planned order level See.

Scenario : After MRP run with scheduling mode 2 - Lead time scheduling.

Inhouse production:

Inside planned order you can see an additional tab called detailed scheduling. This will not be available in basic date scheduling.

Here the capacity of work center is 8 Hr. In routing I mentioned 8hr in each operation. Here 2 quantity so its 16 hrs for each operation. So each operation takes place 2 day in single work center. All together 6 days

Requirement date = 04.10.2024. As per configuration the scheduling will be takes place backwards

Planned end date = Requirement date -  GR processing time (wrk day) = 04.10.24 - 1 = 02.10.24

production end date = Pln end date + Float after production = 02.10.24 - 1 = 01.10.24

Production start date = Production end date - Routing data = 01.10.24 - 6 = 23.09.24

Planned start date = Production start date -  Float before production = 23.09.2024 - 2 = 19.09.2024

Opening date = pln start date - opening period (wrk days) = 19.09.24 - 10 days = 05.09.24 (dummy date no affect on scheduling)

External procurement:

No change as there are no routing.

Here the operation start date of the header material has been passed to the component as the dependent requirement date.

Now let me convert the planned order of header material to production order.

Scheduling in Production.

Here we done the lead time scheduling in planned order. The scheduling in production order also lead time scheduling.

As all the dates are determined in planned order its by lead time scheduling the date from planned order are just copied to production order.

Basic end date = picked from end date of planned order = 02.10.2024

Scheduled end date = Basic end date - float after production = 02.10.2024 - 1 = 30.09.23

Scheduled start date = Scheduled end date - Routing data = 30.10.24 - 6 (2pc*3days) = 23.09.24 

Basic start date = Scheduled start date - Float before production = 23.09.24 - 2 = 19.09.24

Release date = Scheduled start date - release period = 23.09.24 - 5 = 16.09.24 (just time No affect on scheduling)

Conclusion:

I hope this will help you to understand the scheduling in PP. Comment if there are discrepancies. Also have a look to link.

entry.title: Re: SAP MM Career
entry.content: If I learn SAP MM self paced Am I eligible for apply a Exam to get certification?

entry.title: How to Emphasize or add Color Coding to Custom Buttons in Fiori Elements created via Controller Ext.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have created a Fiori Elements Application with the List & Object Page. In the Object Page, we have created two Buttons in header using Controller extension with Page map as shown below.

By default those buttons are not Emphasized or can't add any color for button as shown below.

 

How can we achieve this to Emphasize the button or add some Color as UI Guidelines?

Thanks for feedback

SAP Fiori MakersSAP Fiori Elements

entry.title: Re: this._oApplicationImplementation.oMHFHelper.defineMas...
Re: Adding fields in standard report in public cloud (MB52)
entry.content:
Hello,

You need to make custom fields in the "Custom Fields" application and turn them on for the Fiori app: MB52/Display warehouse stock. You can use BAdi to fill them in. When you find a BAdi that is triggered on save, or whatever place you need to fill those fields in, create an implementation using the 'Custom Logic' application. You can always check, maybe the fields are here, but just hidden.

BR Serhii.
